fact_verdicts.note: Action id b3d452bff7769d7f557ec6b8974760ee6c5e496c276652b654032966621e0ccf#9, type TreasuryWithdrawals, proposed epoch 638, expires after 645 all match the on-chain action header.
body.title matches verbatim, including the truncated '...dApp P...'.
body.abstract states submission by Intersect on behalf of the vendor and that Abstract/Motivation/Rationale were sourced from the approved vendor proposal.
body.motivation: 'requests 1,308,000 ADA (plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve) over 12 months'.
Budget Summary table: 1,635,000 + 49,050 = 1,684,050 ADA, exactly as stated.
onChain.gov_action.rewards has key stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v with value 1684050000000.
Motivation describes an open, standardized interface producing documentation, auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, and live JSON-RPC endpoints.
Motivation: 'Through Catalyst Fund 14, TxPipe has already put the developer layer into production with 5 protocols live on the Tx3 Protocol Registry'.
Motivation: 'funds 12 additional onboardings (3 per quarter)' plus 'development of the MCP server and skill framework'.
Pillar 2 section names Strike Finance, Indigo Protocol, VyFinance, Bodega Market, and Fluid Tokens.
Rationale: 'achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase'.
NCL Compliance section states this nearly verbatim, including 350M NCL covering Epoch 613 to Epoch 713.
Audit & Oversight: audit/oversight costs included within overhead; Intersect administration fee covers administrative oversight.
Audit & Oversight states independent oversight through Intersect and technically capable third-party, with reporting obligations and milestone-based disbursement controls.
Prior Treasury Funding Disclosure: 30 Catalyst proposals across Funds 9–14, 26 delivered, 4 under development and on schedule.
Disclosure names Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC under Intersect's 2025 budget cycle, all being delivered on schedule.
Intersect Budget Management Tooling lists the 2026 TRSC stake address matching stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v.

values_hierarchy.md ranks treasury stewardship/downside protection (2) above evidence quality (3) and public-benefit ecosystem growth (4).
Conflict handling defaults: growth vs. stewardship with weak evidence → prefer ABSTAIN.
Core Position: rejects FCFS models and adopts a rolling-window Net Change Limit framework.
Foundational Axiom: 'Requested ₳ per deliverable should decrease', and budgets for AI-accelerable work should reflect post-AI costs — fair paraphrase of the stated axiom.

verification.summary: All 24 dossier facts are supported by the source material. On-chain header fields, the recipient stake address and lovelace amount, budget figures (1,308,000 + 327,000 contingency; 1,635,000 + 49,050 = 1,684,050), the 67% Hydra Voting threshold, the 350M NCL window (epochs 613–713), the Catalyst track record (30 proposals, 26 delivered, 4 in progress), the on-chain context figures, and the doctrine claims all match exactly. No contradicted or not_found facts, and no invented specifics were detected.
